title: What to know about hiatus hernia before using medicinal herbs
intro: Herbal teas offer a natural and caffeine-free solution for individuals with a hiatus hernia. Chamomile tea, with its anti-inflammatory properties, helps soothe the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. Licorice root tea, rich in glycyrrhizic acid, facilitates the healing of damaged mucous membranes and eases discomfort. Slippery elm bark tea, containing mucilage, forms a protective layer on the lining of the digestive tract, reducing irritation and promoting overall gut health. Lastly, marshmallow root tea, abundant in polysaccharides, provides a soothing coating to the esophagus, offering relief from heartburn and other hiatus hernia symptoms.
definition: A hiatus hernia is a condition in which a portion of the stomach moves upward into the chest cavity through an opening in the diaphragm, called the esophageal hiatus. This occurrence can lead to severe gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) symptoms such as chronic heartburn, chest pain, and difficulty swallowing. If left untreated, it can result in severe complications including esophageal stricture, ulcers, and Barrett's esophagus, which increases the risk of esophageal cancer. Therefore, recognizing and addressing hiatus hernia is crucial for preserving one's digestive health and overall well-being.
